title: Quadratics
summary: Free Cambridge 9709 P1 quadratics — factorisation, completing the square, the discriminant, and quadratic graphs with GeoGebra explorer.
learningObjectives: Complete the square to find vertex form and solve equations.
Use the discriminant to determine the nature of roots.
Sketch quadratic graphs from key features (vertex, intercepts, shape).

simpleExplanation.title: Parabolas everywhere
simpleExplanation.summary: Quadratics model projectile motion, area problems, and optimisation. Master three tools: factorise, formula, and completing the square.
steps: Write in standard form ax² + bx + c = 0 and identify a, b, c.
Try factorisation first when the quadratic factorises cleanly over integers.
Use Δ = b² − 4ac: Δ > 0 two roots, Δ = 0 one repeated root, Δ < 0 no real roots.
Complete the square to find the vertex (h, k) and sketch the parabola.

flashcards.back: Δ = b² − 4ac — sign tells number of real roots.
y = a(x − h)² + k — vertex at (h, k).

sections.content: **Quadratics** open Paper 1 and reappear in disguised form throughout 9709. Cambridge expects clean algebra, **discriminant arguments**, and **sketches** showing intercepts and vertex.
ax² + bx + c = 0 → x = (−b ± √Δ)/2a | Completing the square: x² + bx = (x + b/2)² − (b/2)²

sections.question: Solve 2x² − 5x − 3 = 0 and state the nature of the roots.
sections.solution:
Δ = 25 + 24 = 49 > 0 → two distinct real roots.

Factorise: (2x + 1)(x − 3) = 0

x = **−½** or x = **3**.

sections.content: When asked "show that" an equation has no real roots, compute **Δ < 0** with working — one line of algebra earns the mark; stating the conclusion alone does not.
